title: ucase-authority-v1
description: CI-neutral provenance authority for a proof event. The trust model is CI-NEUTRAL: GitHub Actions is only the reference provider. This record captures WHO/WHERE produced a proof in a provider-agnostic shape (type + provider + best-effort repository/ref/commit/run_id/actor/event), auto-detected from CI env vars (see markers/ciAuthority.ts detectCiAuthority) or supplied explicitly via `prove --authority-file`. It is OPTIONAL on a proof event and ADDITIVE: it does not affect freshness (FRESH matches row/binding/context hashes, not provenance). When present it is built INTO the event before signing, so the signature covers it. `protected_ref` is a tri-state: true/false when the provider exposes an explicit protected-branch signal, and null when unknown.

type.description: "ci" when produced inside a recognised CI provider; "local" otherwise.

provider.description: The CI provider. "generic" is the fallback for local runs or an unrecognised provider.

ref.description: The git ref / branch the run executed against.

commit.description: The commit SHA the run executed against.

run_id.description: The provider's run / pipeline / build identifier.

actor.description: The user/login that triggered the run, when the provider exposes it.

protected_ref.description: Whether the ref is a protected branch. true/false when the provider exposes an explicit signal; null when unknown.

event.description: The provider's triggering event name (e.g. push, pull_request, merge_request).
